title: Lysiloma formosum
taxonomy: Lysiloma formosum is a member of the Fabaceae family. This family, also known as the legume family, comprises a wide variety of flowering plants. The genus Lysiloma is part of the subfamily Faboideae, which is one of the largest subfamilies within the Fabaceae family. The classification of Lysiloma formosum is further supported by its morphological characteristics and genetic studies. By understanding the classification of Lysiloma formosum, researchers can better understand its evolutionary history and relationships with other plant species.

Its bark and leaves are believed to possess antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to treat wounds and reduce swelling.
